description: Letter from Yusuf b. Musa al-Tāhartī, probably from Mahdiyya, to Nahray b. Nissim, Fustat, regarding shipments of goods. Fall 1063. The writer describes difficulties in selling and mentions various goods, including carthamus, camphor, sugar, resin, crocus, indigo, wheat, wool, wood, ruby, beads, pearls, oil and more. Yusuf also discusses trading with Spain and the Maghreb, even though the roads are dangerous, and notes the passing of his brother, Barhun b. Musa. (Information from Gil, Kingdom, Vol. 3, #374) VMR
description_he: מכתב מיוסף בן מוסא אל-טאהרתי, כנראה מאל-מהדייה, לנהראי בן ניסים, פוסטאט, סתיו 1063, על קשיים במכירה וסחורות שונות
